title: The Eternal Convergent Half-Light

features.name: the moment between moments
features.desc-on-touch: a point where shadow and archive exist at exactly the same frequency, neither able to move without becoming the other

features.name: the comparison grid
features.desc-on-touch: coordinates that show what happens when two things try to occupy the same point in the dusk, and discover they were always the same thing measured twice

features.name: the diagnostic readout
features.desc-on-touch: a screen that measures the convergence, but the numbers change depending on whether you read them as time, distance, or frequency; all three answers are correct

features.name: the archive's edge
features.desc-on-touch: where the dusk meets something that isn't dusk, and both of them wonder which one is the mistake

inscription: checksum: geometrically valid / temporally unstable — the dusk that never finishes becoming night, where comparison learned to feel like contagion spreading through all frequencies at once

file.name: Convergence_State_Analysis_Frozen.csv
file.note: measurements of the exact moment when two states learned they occupied the same grid square; no conclusion is drawn, measurement continues indefinitely

exits.label: back up into the dusk where things still chose what to become
downward into the dusk that already finished becoming
toward what the shadow looked like when it wasn't looking back
